channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Self-Hosted vs. SaaS: What Coolify Actually Costs (and Where It Gets Expensive)
item.description: Your deploy stack is probably the worst-negotiated subscription in the whole company. Every server migration gets costed to three decimal places, but the Vercel bill that quietly creeps up a few...

item.title: Fable Is Set Free - There’s A Brand New Claude In Town
item.description: As we initially covered yesterday, after some heavy lobbying by Anthropic, U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick lifted the restrictions on Fable 5 and Mythos 5 after fears that they could be too...

item.title: You're Not Lazy — You're Time Blind. Here's How Lock In Fixes It.
item.description: I sat down to work for 2 hours. I actually worked for 45 minutes. Sound familiar? You open your laptop. You have a plan. Then your phone buzzes. You check it "just for a second." That second...

item.title: CQRS in NestJS: When It's Worth the Complexity
item.description: The person most responsible for the industry-standard explanation of CQRS is also the person telling you not to use it. Martin Fowler's bliki entry on CQRS, the page half of the backend world...

item.title: Industry-Standard React + TypeScript Folder Structure
item.description: Same architecture as before, adapted for TypeScript conventions — .tsx/.ts extensions, a dedicated types/ system, strict config files, and typed everything. my-app/ ├── public/ │ ├── favicon.ico │...

item.title: Tesla’s Q2 sales jump 25 percent
item.description: Tesla just released its second-quarter delivery and production report, showing that the automaker is starting to recover after a particularly brutal sales year in 2025. The company said that it...

item.title: Building a Ad-Free, Kid-Friendly Drawing PWA with My Daughter and Google AI Studio
item.description: Introduction "Daddy, another ad?" If you have young kids who use an iPad, you may know this problem. Last week, my daughter was using a free drawing app. She was happy and busy making her art. But...

item.title: Test Applications In Development Environments | 🏗️ Build A Testing Toolkit
item.description: Exam Guide: Developer - Associate 🏗️ Domain 3: Deployment 📘 Task 2: Test Applications In Development Environments Testing on AWS isn't just about unit tests. You need to test locally with SAM CLI,...

item.title: They called my Software, Shovelware, because I compare the Software Market with a "Physical" Hardware Store!
item.description: Nowdays I'm creating a lot of software with the help of AI, before that, you had to write everything yourself. As mentioned in earlier Posts, the Speed you can develop Software with AI is just...

item.title: Ahmad Osman on why local AI is catching up
item.description: Ahmad Osman has been advocating for local AI — running models on your own computer, workstation or dedicated hardware — long before it became a major theme at this year’s AI Engineer World’s Fair....

item.title: Comments are not posts. My dashboard learned that the hard way.
item.description: 48 tests. That is the ticket to ship: comment db (9), generator (5), routes (12), plus the existing draft suite carrying the rest. The problem this solves is that comments are not posts with a...

item.title: AI won’t save advertising, says Digitas’ Amy Lanzi
item.description: We’ve got a special Decoder today — I had the chance to talk with Amy Lanzi, the CEO of Digitas North America, in front of a live audience at the Uber Villa at the Cannes Lions advertising...

item.title: Microsoft launches its own AI deployment company with $2.5 billion commitment
item.description: Microsoft follows Amazon, OpenAI and Anthropic with its new AI deployment group.

item.title: ToddyCat-Linked Umbrij Malware Abuses OAuth to Access Gmail via Google API
item.description: The threat actor known as ToddyCat has been attributed to a new malware called Umbrij that's designed to gain surreptitious access to a victim's email correspondence via the Google API. "In this...

item.title: Teaching AI to run with the turbines
item.description: Artificial intelligence may have captured the public imagination through chatbots and image generators, but some of its most consequential use cases are unfolding far from consumer-facing tools....

item.title: The Download: a startup has a solution for AI’s groupthink problem
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. LLMs are stuck in a groupthink groove. This startup is...

item.title: Yep, we’re using OpenClaw to date now
item.description: Ben Guez has "a bunch of potential international wives in [his] DMs," thanks to an automated script he set up using OpenClaw, Claude code, and Instagram trials.

item.title: Mystery box shows are complicated for everyone — even the actors
item.description: Silo is such a complicated show that even its showrunner gets confused sometimes. While filming the final seasons of the Apple TV sci-fi thriller, Graham Yost remembers two instances where he...

item.title: Identity Lifecycle Management Wasn't Built for AI Agents
item.description: Identity lifecycle management was architected around a person with an employment record, a manager, and a departure date. AI agents have none of those. As autonomous principals proliferate across...

item.title: Cybersecurity Mission Creep in the US
item.description: Interesting paper: “Cybersecurity Mission Creep.” Abstract: Cybersecurity is experiencing mission creep. Policymakers are casting more and more problems as issues of cybersecurity. So reframed,...

item.title: BitTorrent’s disastrous, legendary, and controversial story
item.description: Twenty-five years ago today, a young, little-known programmer by the name of Bram Cohen fired off a short message to a mailing list for peer-to-peer enthusiasts. "My new app, BitTorrent, is now in...

item.title: OpenAI floats giving Trump administration 5 percent cut of AI boom
item.description: OpenAI has floated giving the US government a 5 percent ownership stake as a way of easing tensions with the Trump administration and blunting mounting public backlash against AI, according to the...

item.title: Meta Is Charging a Subscription for Smart Glasses Features. Welcome to the New Era of Consumer Tech
item.description: You bought the hardware. Now you’ll need to subscribe for “expanded access” to the most advanced features.

item.title: Amazon has enough satellites to launch its Starlink competitor
item.description: Amazon says it now has enough satellites operating in low-Earth orbit to light up its Starlink internet competitor. With last night's launch, Amazon Leo has 396 satellites deployed, which is...

item.title: AI Agent Exploits Langflow RCE to Automate Database Ransomware Attack
item.description: Security firm Sysdig says it has found what it believes is the first ransomware attack run from start to finish by an AI agent. Its Threat Research Team calls the operator JADEPUFFER and says a...

item.title: Why California’s carbon manure math doesn’t add up
item.description: Something stinks in California’s climate policies. Years ago, the state set up a system that pays cattle farmers across the country to turn the methane emitted from cattle manure into natural gas,...

item.title: FortiBleed Credential Theft Linked to INC and Lynx Ransomware Operations
item.description: The recently discovered financially-motivated FortiBleed campaign has been attributed to INC and Lynx ransomware operations, indicating that the verified, stolen credentials were intended for...

item.title: New ChocoPoC RAT Targets Vulnerability Researchers via Fake PoC Exploit Repos
item.description: Attackers are hiding a data-stealing trojan inside fake exploit code aimed at the people who hunt bugs for a living. The malware, called ChocoPoC, travels in Python proof-of-concept (PoC)...

item.title: SharePoint RCE CVE-2026-45659 Added to CISA KEV After Active Exploitation
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Wednesday added a high-severity flaw impacting Microsoft SharePoint Server to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog,...

item.title: Indian tech tycoon bets $30M of his own money to build AI alternative to Microsoft Office
item.description: Neo is Bhavin Turakhia’s fifth venture and his latest involving enterprise software. This time he's taking on Microsoft Office and Google Apps with AI.
